terms.definition: The codified set of written and oral rules governing conduct within an Amish congregation
An institutional decision rule that selectively admits or blocks technologies according to explicit communal values
A discrete chronological interval characterised by a dominant technological interaction mode
The networked resources embedded in social relations that facilitate coordination and cooperation
The measurable stability and density of social bonds within a community
A state of internal cognitive and affective disorder marked by anxiety and overload
Collective trust in the reliability and facticity of shared information
A design orientation that prioritises repairability and longevity of physical artefacts
The intentional engineering of shortened product life cycles to stimulate repeat consumption
The progressive loss of functional order within a closed or semi-closed system; here extended metaphorically to technological and social decay
A system in which outputs feed back to reduce future deviation, stabilising or reinforcing a trend
Structural equivalence across domains allowing analogical mapping
The aggregate capacity of a community to generate and enforce shared moral norms
The systemic drive toward efficiency maximisation that directs technological evolution
Legal framework granting consumers the ability to service and repair purchased hardware and software
